rationale: The homes in this Humble, TX neighborhood (Houston metro, Gulf Coast climate) display classic multi-hip roof forms with short-to-moderate ridgelines relative to overall footprint, consistent with a 6:12 pitch. Ground shadows cast from the eaves are modest in length — not long enough to suggest steep pitches (8:12+), but clearly present, ruling out very shallow slopes (3:12–4:12). The visible roof plane area from the nadir (overhead) view is moderate, indicating a mid-range slope. Shingle texture is visible with mild shadow relief across the planes. Regional norms for suburban Houston-area construction of this style and era strongly favor 5:12–7:12 hip roofs, with 6:12 being the most prevalent standard pitch, making it the best single estimate given all converging visual cues.
